alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment hits 60% U-235; IAEA demands stockpile disclosure
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran has maintained 60% U-235 enrichment with breakout window estimated at 1-2 weeks. IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran declare all uranium stockpiles and grant inspector access to facilities. France and multiple nations warn Iran continues violating NPT commitments. This represents sustained acceleration toward weapons-grade material capability.

alerts.title: Israel-Iran ceasefire unravels; new missile strikes reported amid deal uncertainty
alerts.summary: Incidents feed reports Iran fired missiles at Israel for first time since April ceasefire (Axios, June 12), with Israel responding via air raids on southern Lebanon. Simultaneously, Trump called off threatened strikes citing breakthrough in US-Iran talks, while denying Iran's account of deal terms and labeling Iranians 'dishonorable' (CNBC, June 12). Pakistani PM claimed final peace deal text reached, but diplomatic messaging remains contradictory. Framework appears unstable despite public de-escalation rhetoric.

alerts.title: Ukraine deep-strike attacks intensify against Russia; ~130K-143K Telegram engagement
alerts.summary: Rybar Telegram channel posted multiple high-engagement reports (130K-143K views) on Ukrainian BLA drone strikes into Russia's northwest, including targeting of Leningrad Oblast infrastructure. Posts highlight escalating Ukrainian deep-strike tactics and Russian air defense adaptation challenges. Multiple posts flagged with 'strike' keyword, indicating sustained campaign intensity and tactical evolution mid-2026.

alerts.title: UAE reportedly paid $3B to Iran with $10B more planned to halt attacks
alerts.summary: Reuters report cited by Iran International indicates UAE made $3B payment to Iran and agreed to release additional $10B to halt attacks (incidents feed, June 12). Signals potential third-party financing mechanism to manage escalation, though corroboration from independent sources remains limited. Suggests diplomatic/financial options being explored outside formal US-Iran negotiations.

alerts.title: US sentiment swings negative as Iran tensions dominate discourse (55% negative)
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US news at 55% negative sentiment versus 40% positive, with 'Iran' ranking #10 most-frequent term (~18 mentions) alongside high-frequency political figures (Trump 106x, Judge 56x). Negative polarity shift reflects sustained anxiety over Iran escalation amid mixed diplomatic signals. India sentiment also negative (44%) with 'sailors' and 'death' terms reflecting regional maritime casualty concerns from Hormuz ceasefire breakdown.

analysis_note: Iran maintains 60% uranium enrichment with 1-2 week breakout window while Israel-Iran ceasefire collapses into tit-for-tat strikes; simultaneous US-Iran deal claims lack clarity, leaving threat level at critical (9/10) with proxy escalation ongoing across Lebanon, Eastern Ukraine, and Persian Gulf maritime routes.
